description_long: This object consists of a group of variously sized bottles, arranged in rows. The bottles are predominantly green and brown, with some featuring paper labels that display images and text, possibly in an East Asian script. The largest bottles are demijohn-style with rounded shoulders and cork stoppers, while the smaller bottles are more cylindrical or slightly tapered, also sealed with corks. The labels on the larger bottles are more prominent, with one showing a portrait of a woman in traditional attire. The surfaces of the bottles appear slightly matte and worn, suggesting age or frequent handling. The collection gives the impression of vintage or collectible bottles, possibly for beverages or chemicals.
description: A group of vintage green and brown bottles of varying sizes, many with cork stoppers and paper labels, some featuring portraits and text.
description_view_0: Front view showing the labels on the largest bottles, including a prominent portrait and text, with smaller bottles arranged in rows behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rounded shoulders of the large bottles and the alignment of smaller bottles in straight rows.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the backs of the bottles, with less visible labeling and more focus on the bottle shapes and corks.
description_view_3: Back view of the four largest bottles, with corks visible and minimal label detail, and smaller bottles partially obscured.
attribute_label_or_branding: Several bottles have paper labels, some with red and black text, and at least one with a portrait of a woman in traditional attire. The labels are rectangular and placed on the front of the bottles.
